meta.title: Ecommerce Development Cost: Custom Store Estimate
meta.description: Understand ecommerce development cost for a custom Shopify alternative with Stripe/PayPal, tax, shipping, inventory, ERP sync, coupons, and Core Web Vitals-focused SEO.

hero.h1: ecommerce development cost: estimate your custom online store
hero.subtitle: Plan catalog, checkout, tax, and shipping, then launch a fast, SEO-ready store.

pain.title: Why online stores run over budget
bullets: Have you ever launched a store, then discovered taxes, shipping rules, and refunds were never fully defined
Have you ever added Stripe or PayPal, only to get stuck on webhooks, fraud checks, and failed payment edge cases
Have you ever outgrown your admin panel and inventory process, then paid again to retrofit ERP sync and reporting

benefits.title: A clearer cost breakdown
benefits.desc: You get an estimate tied to scope: catalog size, checkout flows, coupons, shipping logic, taxes, and integrations.

benefits.title: Revenue-ready checkout
benefits.desc: You ship a payment flow with Stripe and PayPal, webhook handling, refunds, and order status you can trust.

benefits.title: SEO and performance built-in
benefits.desc: You launch with technical SEO basics and Core Web Vitals priorities so pages load fast and rank better.

scope.title: What’s included in ecommerce development
bullets: Cart and checkout with Stripe and PayPal
Tax rules, invoices, and refund handling
Shipping rates, zones, and tracking emails
Admin panel for products, orders, and customers
ERP integration option for sync and reporting
SEO setup, structured data, and Core Web Vitals

useCases.title: Shopify alternative
useCases.desc: You need a custom store without platform limits, with full ownership of features and data.

useCases.title: Headless ecommerce
useCases.desc: You want a flexible frontend with a robust backend and APIs for catalog, cart, and orders.

useCases.title: B2B ordering
useCases.desc: You support price lists, customer groups, purchase orders, and admin-approved workflows.

useCases.title: Subscription or bundles
useCases.desc: You sell recurring products or bundles with clear pricing, renewals, and order history.

useCases.title: ERP-first ops
useCases.desc: You need inventory, orders, and customers to sync with your ERP to reduce manual work.

stack.title: Build options for your online store

options.name: Custom storefront
options.note: A tailored experience with full control over SEO, performance, and conversion flows.

options.name: Headless CMS
options.note: Manage content and landing pages while keeping ecommerce logic in a dedicated backend.

options.name: Stripe + PayPal
options.note: Payment methods with webhook-driven order states, refunds, and fraud-aware handling.

options.name: ERP integration
options.note: Sync products, stock, pricing, and orders with your operational system when needed.

options.name: Admin panel
options.note: Operate catalog, inventory, coupons, and fulfillment with role-based access and audit trails.

steps.title: Define scope
steps.desc: You share catalog needs, integrations, and rules for tax, shipping, and discounts.

steps.title: Estimate and plan
steps.desc: You receive milestones, deliverables, and a cost range based on the agreed scope.

steps.title: Build and launch
steps.desc: You get a tested store with an admin panel, analytics hooks, and a deployment checklist.

faq.q: What affects ecommerce development cost the most?
faq.a: Scope and complexity: catalog rules, shipping and tax logic, payment edge cases, coupons, admin workflows, and external integrations like an ERP.

faq.q: Can you support Stripe and PayPal together?
faq.a: Yes. We can offer both, including webhook handling for payment confirmation, refunds, dispute flows, and consistent order status updates.

faq.q: Do you handle taxes and shipping configuration?
faq.a: Yes. We implement the rules you need for regions, rates, carriers, and checkout messaging, and document how your team updates them in the admin panel.

faq.q: Will my store be SEO-ready from day one?
faq.a: Yes. We cover technical SEO foundations like clean URLs, metadata, sitemaps, structured data where relevant, and Core Web Vitals priorities.

faq.q: Is headless ecommerce right for my business?
faq.a: Headless can help when you need a custom frontend or multiple channels. If your needs are simpler, a conventional architecture can be faster to ship and cheaper to maintain.

ctaFinal.title: Get a realistic ecommerce cost estimate
ctaFinal.desc: Share your catalog, payment, tax, and shipping needs and get a clear scope and budget.
